name: Coq Au Vin
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Rinse the chicken and pat dry thoroughly.
3. Melt 2 tablespoons margarine in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
4. Add garlic powder and herbs; stir together for 30 seconds.
5. Dredge the chicken pieces in flour, shaking off any excess.
6. Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned on both sides. Remove to a small casserole dish and set aside.
7. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons margarine to the skillet.
8. Add mushrooms and onions; sauté until golden brown.
9. Add the onions and mushrooms to the casserole dish with the chicken.
10. Pour in the white wine and scrape up any browned pieces from the bottom of the skillet.
11. Pour the wine over the chicken.
12. Cover and bake, uncovered, for 50 to 60 minutes or until the chicken is tender.
